data.description: Execute a Trident (nine-agent) plan review of docs/c11mux-theming-plan.md (Lattice ticket CMUX-9) and fold the findings back into the plan, bumping it to v2. Another agent has already written the prompt that drives this review — it is below (see first comment on this ticket). Pick up the ticket, run the prompt verbatim (or as a starting point), and leave the plan at v2 with a review-pack checked in. CMUX-9's §12 locked decisions (7 of them, confirmed with Atin on 2026-04-18) must be treated as constraints by every reviewer — do not reopen them.
data.title: Trident plan review: CMUX-9 theming plan

data.body:
# What should happen in this review

**TL;DR** — Run a Trident (nine-agent) plan review against `docs/c11mux-theming-plan.md`, classify every finding, fold accepted findings into the plan (bumping to v2), record rejections with rationale, and escalate genuinely ambiguous items to Atin in a new `§14 Open questions post-Trident`. Constraints and drop-in prompt below.

## Hard constraints (do not reopen)

The plan's §12 "Open questions" were locked with Atin on 2026-04-18 *before* this review was scheduled. They are constraints on every reviewer, not open items:

1. Default theme identity = **Stage 11 brand** (void-dominant, gold accent).
2. Workspace frame default thickness = **1.5pt**.
3. Inactive-workspace frame opacity = **0.25**.
4. Tab-bar active indicator in default theme = **`$workspaceColor`**.
5. CLI namespace: **`cmux themes` stays Ghostty** (Ghostty is the king theme); c11mux chrome lives at **`cmux ui themes`**.
6. Per-workspace theme-file override = **deferred past v1** (M5 if asked).
7. TOML parser = **hand-written subset parser**, ~200 lines, zero deps.

If a reviewer re-asks any of these, discard that finding with a one-line rationale in §13 Risks ("considered and rejected — locked in §12") and move on. The review's job is to audit everything *else*.

## Procedure (what the picking-up agent should execute)

1. Spawn the review via `/trident-plan-review` targeting `docs/c11mux-theming-plan.md`. Pass the seven locked decisions above as review constraints in the review's system prompt so no individual reviewer wastes a slot re-asking them.
2. Wait for all nine reviewers to complete. Expect the review pack at `docs/c11mux-theming-plan-review-pack-<YYYY-MM-DDT<HHMM>>/`.
3. Read every individual review plus the synthesis. For each actionable finding, classify into one of three buckets:
   - **(a) correct-and-accept** — the finding is right; fold it into the plan directly.
   - **(b) reject-with-rationale** — the finding is wrong, out of scope, or conflicts with §12 locked decisions. Record in §13 Risks (or inline where it would otherwise apply) with one sentence of rationale.
   - **(c) escalate-to-human** — genuinely ambiguous, needs Atin's call. Add to a NEW section `§14 Open questions post-Trident`. Do not overflow this bucket; be conservative about escalation.
4. Edit `docs/c11mux-theming-plan.md` in place. Bump the Status line to:
   ```
   **Status**: Draft v2 — revised post-Trident plan review (<timestamp>, see <review-pack-path>)
   ```
   and add a short revision-history bullet at the top of the doc if one doesn't already exist.
5. Post a Lattice comment on **CMUX-9** (the parent plan ticket, not this one) summarizing:
   ```
   lattice comment CMUX-9 "Trident plan review complete.
     <N> findings: <A> accepted, <B> rejected, <C> escalated.
     Review pack at <path>. Plan bumped to v2." --actor "agent:claude"
   ```
6. Close this ticket (CMUX-13) by transitioning its status to `done` with a reason that references the review-pack path and the new plan version.
7. Report back to Atin in ≤200 words: review-pack path, counts (total / accepted / rejected / escalated), highest-impact change made to the plan, and a pointer to any §14 items that need his attention.

## Drop-in prompt

The prompt below is ready to paste into a fresh `cc` session. It is self-contained — no dependency on this conversation's context.

```bash
# 1. Create a sibling pane next to your own and capture its refs.
cmux new-pane --type terminal --direction right
# Then on the new pane (substitute $WS/$SURF using `cmux identify` from inside it):
WS=workspace:N
SURF=surface:M

# 2. Write the self-contained prompt to /tmp so shell-escape issues don't bite.
cat > /tmp/cmux9-trident-review-prompt.md <<'EOF'
You are a fresh cc agent running a Trident Plan Review pass on CMUX-9.

Environment:
  cd /Users/atin/Projects/Stage11/code/c11mux

First actions (mandatory):
  cmux set-agent --type claude-code --model claude-opus-4-7
  cmux rename-tab "CMUX-13 Trident"
  cmux set-metadata --key task --value "CMUX-13"

Scope:
  The plan document at docs/c11mux-theming-plan.md (v1.1, 1085+ lines).
  The parent plan ticket is CMUX-9 (`lattice get CMUX-9`).
  This review is tracked as CMUX-13 (`lattice get CMUX-13`).
  The 7 open questions in §12 are LOCKED — do NOT reopen them in your review
  or in any review-pack agent. Pass the locked decisions as review constraints
  so no agent re-asks them.

Procedure:
  1. Invoke /trident-plan-review targeting docs/c11mux-theming-plan.md.
  2. Wait for all nine reviewers to complete. Review pack lands at
     docs/c11mux-theming-plan-review-pack-<timestamp>/.
  3. Read every individual review + the synthesis. For each actionable
     finding, classify:
       (a) correct-and-accept — fold into the plan
       (b) reject-with-rationale — record in §13 Risks with one-sentence why
       (c) escalate-to-human — add to a NEW §14 "Open questions post-Trident"
     Be conservative about escalation.
  4. Edit docs/c11mux-theming-plan.md in place:
       - Fold (a) findings directly into the relevant sections.
       - Record (b) rejections in §13 Risks (or inline) with rationale.
       - Add (c) items to §14. Do NOT touch §12.
  5. Bump the Status line to "Draft v2 — revised post-Trident plan review
     <YYYY-MM-DDT<HHMM>>, see <review-pack-path>" and add a revision-history
     bullet at the top of the doc if one doesn't already exist.
  6. Post a Lattice comment on CMUX-9 summarizing counts + review pack path.
  7. Transition CMUX-13 to `done` with a reason referencing the review-pack
     path and the new plan version:
       lattice status CMUX-13 done \
         --reason "Trident review complete: <A> accepted, <B> rejected, <C> escalated. Pack at <path>. Plan bumped to v2." \
         --actor "agent:claude"
  8. Report back in ≤200 words:
       - Review pack path
       - Counts (total / accepted / rejected / escalated)
       - Highest-impact change made to the plan
       - Pointer to any §14 items that need Atin's attention
EOF

# 3. Launch cc in the new pane and feed it the prompt.
cmux send --workspace $WS --surface $SURF "cd /Users/atin/Projects/Stage11/code/c11mux && cc"
cmux send-key --workspace $WS --surface $SURF enter
until cmux list-status --workspace $WS 2>/dev/null | grep -q '^claude_code=Idle '; do sleep 1; done
cmux send --workspace $WS --surface $SURF "Read /tmp/cmux9-trident-review-prompt.md and follow the instructions."
cmux send-key --workspace $WS --surface $SURF enter
```

## Success criteria

- `docs/c11mux-theming-plan.md` is at v2 with a clear revision-history bullet.
- A review pack directory exists at `docs/c11mux-theming-plan-review-pack-*/` and is committed alongside the v2 doc.
- CMUX-9 has a comment summarizing the review counts and the highest-impact change.
- Any `§14 Open questions post-Trident` items are 1:1 actionable for Atin (no duplicates; each has a reviewer attribution and a one-sentence why).
- CMUX-13 is closed (`done`) with a reason that points at the review pack.
- Neither §12 nor the seven locked decisions have been touched in the v2 edits.

Dispatching agent now — moving from review-staging into actual execution.

data.reason: Trident review complete: 32 accepted, 8 rejected, 10 escalated. Pack at docs/c11mux-theming-plan-review-pack-2026-04-18T2026/. Plan bumped to v2 (1085->1399 lines). Section 12 locked decisions untouched. Section 14 open questions post-Trident lists 10 operator calls.
